summary: Russia launched a large-scale combined night attack on Ukraine using 2 anti-ship missiles (Zircon/Oniks), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones, with confirmed strikes at 11 locations and debris falls at 13 locations. Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, and Kharkiv regions, while ballistic missile threats were repeatedly issued for Kyiv, Kharkiv, Chuhuiv, and other cities from launch sites in Bryansk Oblast and Taganrog. Russian forces reportedly liberated the villages of Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region, and struck a Ukrainian training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with 2 Zircon/Oniks anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with one missile and 14 strike drones hitting 11 locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk region and separately on Donetsk and Kharkiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/66662, @kpszsu/66685).

Multiple ballistic missile threats were issued for Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Chuhuiv from launch areas in Bryansk Oblast and Taganrog, Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68157, @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68155).
Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia city with KAB bombs, injuring three people including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).
Russian MoD reported liberation of Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221).
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting boat and unmanned craft depots and personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).
Approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk area toward Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).

Drones were tracked across multiple frontline regions including Kharkiv (Staryi Merchik, Liubotyn, Chuhuiv, Zmiiv), Chernihiv (Kholmiv, Ponornytsia, Novhorod-Siverskyi, Korukivka, Berezna, Semenivka), Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kropyvnytskyi directions (Source: @eRadarrua/98571, @eRadarrua/98532, @eRadarrua/98535, @eRadarrua/98540, @kpszsu/66673)

sections.executive_summary: Russia conducted a major combined overnight strike on Ukraine employing 2 Zircon/Oniks anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with confirmed hits at 11 locations and debris falls at 13 locations. Russian tactical aviation simultaneously deployed guided aviation bombs against Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, and Kharkiv regions, while ballistic missile launch threats were repeatedly detected from Bryansk Oblast and Taganrog targeting Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Chuhuiv. On the ground, Russian forces reported capturing Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region, and struck a Ukrainian training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones.

points: Ukraine's Air Force reported that Russia launched 2 anti-ship missiles (Zircon/Oniks), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones in a night attack; one missile and 14 strike drones were confirmed to have hit 11 locations, with debris from intercepted missiles/drones falling at 13 locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Zaporizhzhia city was struck with KAB guided bombs, injuring three people including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).

Dnipropetrovsk region was attacked nearly 20 times by drones and artillery, with three gas stations set on fire, a sports school ignited, and two people injured, according to the regional administration (Source: @UkraineNow/82233).

Kyiv was hit by ballistic missile(s), with fires reported at multiple addresses in the Darnytskyi district including near a residential building, a car service station, and a non-residential building; two people were reported injured as of morning (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).

The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead; the 'South' operational command reported the death and stated the cause is being established (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31226).

Ukraine's position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate, with Russian troops moving closer to Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, according to Bloomberg reporting citing military analyst Michael Kofman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214).

Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko announced Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029 following meetings with UK and Lithuanian finance ministers at a forum in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
Russian MoD reported that Russian forces liberated the villages of Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221).

Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting depots of boats and unmanned craft as well as personnel; the Russian MoD released objective monitoring footage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

Putin acknowledged that Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure have caused deficits but stated they are not critical; he also announced the need to ramp up production and supply of air defense systems (Source: @tass_agency/382809, @tass_agency/382810).
Putin stated that Crimea's fuel needs will be met, with current reserves lasting several days, and that supplies will be strengthened by land and sea routes (Source: @tass_agency/382812).
Traffic on the Kerch Bridge was temporarily closed, according to the situation channel on approaches to the bridge (Source: @tass_agency/382805).
Emergency power outages affected a significant portion of Zaporizhzhia region on the Russian-controlled side, according to Balitsky (Source: @tass_agency/382835).

Putin stated Russia will not give Ukraine any chance to halt the advance of Russian forces and expressed readiness to continue discussing all modalities from the Anchorage agreements with the US (Source: @tass_agency/382825, @tass_agency/382831).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Donetsk and Kharkiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/66685).
Ballistic missile threats were issued for Kharkiv and Chuhuiv in Kharkiv region, with launch warnings from Bryansk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68157, @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68136).

Multiple drone groups were tracked across Kharkiv region heading toward Staryi Merchik, Liubotyn, Chuhuiv, Zmiiv, Lozova, Nova Vodolaha, and Lymanivka from various directions (Source: @eRadarrua/98571, @eRadarrua/98564, @kpszsu/66691, @kpszsu/66665, @kpszsu/66672).
Russian MoD reported liberation of Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221).
Ukraine's position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate with Russian forces approaching Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, per Bloomberg and analyst Michael Kofman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214).
Drones were tracked on the border of Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions heading toward Orilka, and 4 groups were reported on that regional boundary (Source: @kpszsu/66688, @eRadarrua/98572).
A reactive-type drone was tracked on the border of Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/66688).
Ballistic missile threats were repeatedly issued for Kyiv from Bryansk Oblast, with multiple alerts for Iskander-M launches (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68142, @mon1tor_ua/68145).

Kyiv was struck by ballistic missile(s), with fires in Darnytskyi district at multiple addresses including near a residential building, a car service station, and a non-residential building; two people injured (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).

Drones were tracked across Chernihiv region in the areas of Kholmiv, Ponornytsia, Novhorod-Siverskyi, Korukivka, Berezna, Semenivka, Horodnia, Parafiivka, and Ichnia, with groups of 4 and 6 reported (Source: @eRadarrua/98532, @eRadarrua/98535, @eRadarrua/98540, @eRadarrua/98569, @eRadarrua/98519).
Drones moved from Sumy region toward Chernihiv region in the direction of Novhorod-Siverskyi, and were also tracked near Boryny in Sumy region heading west (Source: @kpszsu/66659, @kpszsu/66658).
A reactive-type drone was tracked in Sumy region heading toward Terny (Source: @kpszsu/66664, @kpszsu/66684).
Drones were tracked in the south of Chernihiv region heading toward Poltava region in the direction of Pyriatyn (Source: @kpszsu/66695).
Russian tactical aviation was active on the northeastern and southeastern directions with a threat of aviation strike means for frontline regions (Source: @kpszsu/66696, @kpszsu/66690).
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting depots of boats and unmanned craft as well as personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).
Russian MoD reported liberation of Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221).
Zaporizhzhia city was struck with KAB guided bombs, injuring three people including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).
Drones were tracked heading toward Zaporizhzhia city from the northeast, and in Zaporizhzhia region toward Vilnyansk (Source: @kpszsu/66692, @eRadarrua/98574).
Drones were tracked in Dnipropetrovsk region toward Pavlohrad and Synelnykove, and in the north of Dnipropetrovsk region near Yurivka heading west (Source: @eRadarrua/98574, @kpszsu/66660).
Approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk area (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Emergency power outages affected a significant portion of Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia region, according to Balitsky (Source: @tass_agency/382835).

Russia launched a combined night strike with 2 Zircon/Oniks anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones; one missile and 14 strike drones hit 11 locations, with debris at 13 locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Dnipropetrovsk region and separately on Donetsk and Kharkiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/66662, @kpszsu/66685).

Multiple ballistic missile launch threats were detected from Bryansk Oblast targeting Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Chuhuiv, and from Taganrog in Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68155, @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68157, @mon1tor_ua/68137).
Approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk area toward Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Russian tactical aviation was active on both northeastern and southeastern directions, with warnings of aviation strike means for frontline regions (Source: @kpszsu/66696, @kpszsu/66690).

Drones of various types were tracked across at least 8 oblasts including Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Kirovograd regions, with reconnaissance, strike, and reactive variants reported (Source: @eRadarrua/98571, @eRadarrua/98532, @kpszsu/66664, @kpszsu/66673).
Russian forces used Geranium drones to strike a Ukrainian training camp in Mykolaiv region, targeting boat depots, unmanned craft depots, and personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

key_developments: Russia conducted a major combined overnight attack using 2 Zircon/Oniks anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with confirmed hits at 11 locations and debris falls at 13 locations across Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk region and separately on Donetsk and Kharkiv regions, indicating continued use of air-dropped munitions against frontline and rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66662, @kpszsu/66685).

Multiple ballistic missile launch threats were issued from Bryansk Oblast targeting Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Chuhuiv, and from Taganrog in Rostov region, demonstrating Russia's continued use of Iskander-M systems from multiple launch corridors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68155, @mon1tor_ua/68137).

Russian MoD reported the liberation of Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia region, suggesting incremental Russian territorial gains in the southern operational direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221).

Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting depots of boats and unmanned craft as well as personnel, indicating targeting of Ukraine's riverine and USV capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

Approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk area, while drone activity was tracked across Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Kirovograd regions, showing sustained multi-axis UAV pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/98563, @eRadarrua/98571).

Kyiv was struck by ballistic missile(s) with fires in Darnytskyi district at multiple addresses including near a residential building, a car service station, and a non-residential building, with two people injured; Zaporizhzhia was also hit with KABs injuring three including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82231, @UkraineNow/82236).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating significantly, with Russia conducting one of its larger combined strikes in recent weeks using cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, and over 140 drones in a single night. The repeated ballistic missile launch warnings from both Bryansk Oblast and Taganrog indicate Russia maintains the ability to threaten multiple major Ukrainian cities simultaneously from different axes. The Russian MoD's claim of capturing Pisantsy and Novoselovka, combined with Bloomberg's reporting on deteriorating Ukrainian positions in Donetsk region near Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, suggests Russia is maintaining ground pressure in the east while conducting strategic strikes nationwide. The targeting of a Ukrainian training camp in Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones indicates Russia is prioritizing disruption of Ukraine's force generation and asymmetric naval capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Putin publicly acknowledged that Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are causing deficits, though he characterized them as non-critical, while simultaneously calling for increased production of air defense systems — an implicit admission of ongoing vulnerability. The Kerch Bridge was temporarily closed, though the cause is unspecified in the messages. Emergency power outages affected a significant portion of Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia region. On the diplomatic front, Trump reportedly raised the possibility of revising the Anchorage agreements at the G7 summit, signaling potential shifts in US policy that had previously accepted Russian control of Donbas in any peace deal. Ukraine's Finance Minister announced a need for nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029, underscoring continued heavy reliance on Western economic support.
sections.intelligence_notes: The death of the 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade commander Vladimir Kononnikov, reported by the 'South' operational command, is a notable leadership loss with an undisclosed cause that warrants monitoring for possible operational security implications. The large volume of drone tracks across Chernihiv and Sumy regions, combined with Shahed-136 launches from Kursk, suggests Russia may be probing northern air defenses as part of a broader saturation strategy. The Anchorage agreement revision signal from Trump represents a potentially significant diplomatic development, though its operational impact remains unclear. The Russian acknowledgment of energy infrastructure damage, even while downplaying it, combined with the Kerch Bridge closure and Zaporizhzhia blackouts, suggests Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics and energy networks are having measurable effects.
